reasoning: The short-term changes in the option-adjusted spread (OAS) of the ICE BofA Corporate Index closely resemble a random walk, historically displaying a near 50/50 chance of increasing or decreasing over short horizons. The FRED statistical tool provides a combined estimate of 49% for a near-term increase (e.g., around early/mid-March). Spreads have drifted upward recently (reaching 0.86 in late February 2026 from 0.76 earlier in the month) and remain near all-time tights, introducing a structural asymmetry that favors mild widening (increases) over the medium term. Therefore, for the closer horizon (March 8, one week out), the linear trend points to minor mean-reversion slightly downward (49%), while by March 31, the positive drift and historical tightness suggest a slightly higher probability of an overall increase (51%). As uncertainty compounds, probabilities remain anchored near 50%.
